page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance provides 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, delivering a total of 16 vCPUs. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource sharing at the CPU level.

The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 4.0 GB of memory allocated per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated core model offers predictable execution times for workloads that do not benefit from hyperthreading. The lack of bundled local storage and GPU hardware positions this instance as a cost-efficient option for standard compute tasks where external block storage is preferred. Tradeoffs include the absence of local NVMe drives and hardware accelerators, which limits its utility for high-speed local caching or parallelized graphics processing. Suitable workloads include general-purpose applications, web serving, and distributed enterprise applications requiring dedicated CPU execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at 3.2 GHz, providing 16 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core for a total of 16 vCPUs. The instance features 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without hyperthreading, such as web serving, application servers, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
